---
title: 'What''s your story? Why are you here?'
date: '2019-12-17T06:35:00.0040000+00:00'
draft: false
categories:
  - 'Musings'
---

Dear Jesus,<br />
<br />
I sit here at stare at the blank page on my screen. Hesitant. Unsure about what to write. I find it acts as a strong metaphor for my life at the moment.<br />
<br />
One giant blank page. Thousands of potential things I "could" do; yet, none of them seem viable or worthy of pursuit.<br />
<br />
I find myself lacking a larger narrative for my life, a drive, a vision, a purpose.<br />
<br />
<ul>
<li>I know my calling is to those who are distressed, discouraged, and in debt; to train them into men and women who can be mighty for God, pulling down strongholds. These battles will take place in the hearts and minds of men.&nbsp;</li>
<li>I know I am a Storyteller. Writer/Scribe. Thinker, Sojourner. I am someone who works to discover the truth of a matter and bring it to the light. NO HIDING is my life's motto.&nbsp;</li>
<li>I know I am a father of two boys and they are my primary ministry and calling at this time. Yet, if I'm not headed anywhere, can I lead them?</li>
</ul>
<br />
<a href="https://www.entreleadership.com/blog/podcasts/donald-miller-core-values" target="_blank">Donald Miller</a> talked in the StoryBrand Podcast about creating a Family Mission Statement. He sold the idea that the mission statement doesn't have to be long term or life long. It can be a short-term mission statement. "We are a family that gets rid of our excess and sells it in a garage sale this spring..." and then you review it often and the whole family assesses what they've been doing to reach that target.<br />
<br />
So what is our vision? What is our family mission statement?<br />
<br />
Elements of a good mission statement:<br />
<br />
<ul>
<li><b>Identify the problem that we are on Earth to solve.</b></li>
<ul>
<li></li>
<li><i>"Most people suffer with..."</i></li>
<li>Be Specific. Don't be vague. They should immediately feel connected to the problem.&nbsp;</li>
</ul>
<li><b>How do we solve the problem?</b></li>
<ul>
<li></li>
<li><i>"We have a product/process that does..."</i></li>
<li>Make it feel like a new idea. Make it understandable. Make it brief.&nbsp;</li>
<li>This is the product, even if you sell a thousand things, make it one thing.&nbsp;</li>
</ul>
<li><b>What is their happy ending?</b></li>
<ul>
<li><i>"... so that they (happy ending)."</i></li>
<li>What is the outcome? How do they look when we're brought the solution to them?</li>
<li>This should be the controlling idea, everything drives to this.</li>
<li>Your product is not your business, their happy ended is the what you are selling. That's what you are in the business of doing.&nbsp;</li>
</ul>
</ul>
<br />
I don't have answers yet... just throwing this out there to ponder.<br />
<br />
Here's a quick possibility:<br />
<br />
<br />
<ul>
<li>Many people are walking through life "faking it till they make", lost in their own life story.</li>
</ul>
<ul>
<li>I write stories and articles that help people see past the lies and discover their true selves.</li>
</ul>
<ul>
<li>So they can live more authentically free to be who they were created to be.&nbsp;</li>
</ul>
<br />
Needs work... but it's a start.<br />
<br />
<br />
<b>What about you?&nbsp;</b><br />
<br />
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
<i>What problem are you on Earth to solve?</i>&nbsp;</blockquote>
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
<i>How do you solve it?</i>&nbsp;</blockquote>
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
<i>What is the happy ending others receive from your solution?</i></blockquote>
